title: Pitcairnia sanguinea
intro: Pitcairnia sanguinea, commonly known as the Red Plume or Red Bromeliad, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Pitcairnia sanguinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Pitcairnia sanguinea is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. The Bromeliaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, consisting of around 3,000 species, and is native to the Americas. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Pitcairnia sanguinea belonging to the Pitcairnioideae subfamily. The subfamily Pitcairnioideae is a group of tropical plants that are typically epiphytic or terrestrial, and are found in a wide range of habitats. Pitcairnia sanguinea's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the broader bromeliad family.
